source: Official patch notes and mechanic patch-history references

Definition.text: Exposure is a resistance-reducing debuff that applies a fixed negative modifier to affected elemental resistance.
Definition.source: official patch-note discussions on Elemental Exposure

Purpose.text: Reduce enemy elemental resistance before penetration-style effects for subsequent elemental damage interaction.

Core Rules.text: Exposure is applied as a resistance reduction effect and is constrained by source rules and level constraints in affected patch contexts.

Internal Rules.text: Certain systems define minimum exposure strength floors and source limitations in specific mastery or patch rule sets.

Calculation Order.text: 1) source applies exposure debuff 2) resistance reduction is accounted for 3) penetration and other downstream modifiers apply against adjusted resistance state.
Calculation Order.source: patch discussion stating exposure reduced resistance before penetration

Priority Rules.text: Exposure reduction interacts before other penetration checks; where explicitly stated, Exposure may use minimum floor rules and is also affected by effect reduction on the player.

Preconditions.text: A skill or curse able to inflict Exposure must successfully apply to a qualifying enemy within patch-enforced level or source limits.

Postconditions.text: Affected resistance is reduced, potentially lowering final elemental resistance outcomes for subsequent damage and ailment processing.

Limitations.text: Exposure effect size can be reduced by enemy rarity/class settings in specific patches and can be reduced for the player by relevant mastery rules.

Caps.text: Patch contexts can define minimum floor values and reduced max-impact multipliers; exact effective caps are not globally uniform in sources used in this batch.

Formula.text: No single standalone formula in sourced texts. Apply Exposure as a resistance modifier term, then run remaining resistance checks including penetration and damage scaling.

prevents: over-reduction via raw penetration alone when resistance floor reached
Related Mechanics.source: official patch notes and follow-up references

text: Map mod of Exposure history conflicts in effect values across patches
player-effect reduction and enemy-effect application rules
Patch-specific masteries and map modifiers can override base exposure strength behavior.
Exceptions.source: 3.21.0 patch notes and map mod notes

Hidden Rules.text: Model Exposure with explicit patch/version branch because historical patches changed relative strengths and application constraints.

Developer Notes.text: Keep Exposure and penetration as separate rule planes with ordered application metadata to preserve 'before penetration' constraints in evaluator logic.

Historical Changes.text: Patch 0.3.0 changed notable properties of Elemental Exposure application and level constraints. Later patch notes introduced floor and effect-reduction variants in explicit contexts.

text.note: Exposure stated to reduce enemy resistances before penetration and gained level constraints for curse/infliction contexts.
"of Exposure" map mod values adjusted to lower magnitude in mid/high tier variants.
Mastery context defines floor and 50% reduced effect of Exposure on player-side.

text: A curse or hit-effect that inflicts Exposure lowers resistance first, then penetration is applied afterward.
Map mods using of Exposure apply adjusted maximum resistance reductions in patch-specific values.
Exposure and penetration are interchangeable effects
Exposure always stacks linearly with all forms of resistance reduction without order constraints

cannot_affect: chaos resistance without explicit source rules
